title: Listen and Choose a Response — Academic Life

questions.stem: Professor, I didn't quite follow the assignment. Could you explain it again?

options.A: I'm glad you asked. The key requirement is a 10-page analysis.
options.B: The assignment is due next Friday, yes.
options.C: You should read chapter 5 for background.

questions.explanation: The student is asking for clarification about the assignment itself, so re-explaining the requirements is the most helpful response.

questions.stem: I'm heading to the coffee shop. Can I get you anything?

options.B: Yes, a latte would be great. Here's some cash.
options.C: The coffee shop closes at 6 PM.

questions.explanation: The speaker is offering to bring something back, so accepting the offer and specifying what you'd like is the natural response.

questions.stem: Do you really think we can finish this research paper by Friday?

options.A: Friday is the last day of the week.
options.B: If we split the work, I think we can make it.
options.C: Research papers require at least five sources.

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing doubt about the deadline, so offering a practical solution addresses their concern constructively.

questions.stem: We're having a study group session tonight. Want to join us?

options.A: I appreciate the offer, but I have a part-time job tonight.
options.B: Study groups are very effective for exam preparation.
options.C: Yes, I finished studying for the exam.

questions.explanation: The speaker is inviting you to join, so politely declining with a reason is appropriate rather than ignoring the invitation.

questions.stem: I can't decide between taking economics or psychology this semester. What do you think?

options.A: Both are offered in the fall semester.
options.B: Well, what are you more interested in?
options.C: Economics 101 has a very strict professor.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking for advice on a personal preference, so helping them think through their interests is more helpful than giving unsolicited opinions.

questions.stem: Thank you so much for reviewing my draft. Your feedback was really helpful.

options.A: You're welcome. I'm looking forward to the final version.
options.B: Yes, the draft did need quite a bit of work.
options.C: I submitted my draft last week.

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing gratitude, so a gracious acknowledgment and encouragement is the polite response.

questions.stem: I'm looking for the science building. Is it near here?

options.A: The science department is offering several new courses.
options.B: Yes, it's the red brick building right past the fountain.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking for directions, so providing clear location information directly addresses their need.

questions.stem: I think we should move our meeting to Thursday instead. Does that work for everyone?

options.A: Thursday is the day after Wednesday.
options.B: I have a class until 3 PM, but anytime after that works.
options.C: We met last Thursday to discuss the project.

questions.explanation: The speaker is proposing a schedule change and asking for availability, so stating your availability is the most appropriate response.

audioSegments.text: Professor, I didn't quite follow the assignment. Could you explain it again?
I'm heading to the coffee shop. Can I get you anything?
Do you really think we can finish this research paper by Friday?
We're having a study group session tonight. Want to join us?
I can't decide between taking economics or psychology this semester. What do you think?
Thank you so much for reviewing my draft. Your feedback was really helpful.
I'm looking for the science building. Is it near here?
I think we should move our meeting to Thursday instead. Does that work for everyone?
